title: cotinus coggygria var. purpureus
intro: Cotinus coggygria var. purpureus, also known as the Purple Smoke Bush, is a deciduous shrub native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia, characterized by its stunning purple foliage and wispy, smoky plumes. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Cotinus coggygria var. purpureus include reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and providing antioxidant protection against oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional remedies and folk medicine.
